notes: Phase M — the ONE model-choice policy for the harness (HarnessModelPolicyService). Per-surface x per-CLI model + the API provider order, all zero-rebuild. Resolution order: user override (Models UI) -> Best-Quality upgrade (claude build/iterate only) -> content (below) -> CLI native default. Gated by the harness_model_policy flag (default OFF); when OFF the harness uses the legacy AutoFixCLIPolicyService pins. NEVER auto-selects fable (too expensive) - fable is opt-in via an explicit user override only. RULE #1: per-CLI flag syntax lives in flagTemplate here, not in the binary.

claude.rationale: Tiny single-file JS fixes: haiku benchmarked fastest (2026-06-01). Not upgraded by Best Quality - autofix is speed-sensitive, not the quality-sensitive path.

In Best Quality routing mode, claude build+iterate surfaces upgrade to opus. Fable is intentionally NOT the auto-upgrade target (very expensive, burns tokens); a user can still pin fable per-surface via the Models UI.

_apiProviderOrderNote: Delivery audit / vision-judge provider order. Replaces the hardcoded [.google,.openAI,.anthropic,.perplexity] order in detectDefaultProvider (a RULE #1 in-binary provider preference). Reorder here to change which provider judges CLI output, zero-rebuild.
_modelChoicesNote: The model options the Models UI picker offers per CLI ([value, label]; value '' = default). A CLI with a REAL curated list here (more than a lone Default) uses it verbatim - that's why claude keeps its family ALIASES (opus/sonnet/haiku auto-follow the newest of each family via the claude CLI). A CLI NOT listed here (codex today) is populated DYNAMICALLY from the weekly OpenRouter catalog (llm-catalog/providers/<provider>.json): claude<-anthropic, codex<-openai, filtered to real+live ids (directApiName present, heartbeat not-failed) so the value is a valid `--model`. So a new OpenAI model auto-appears in the codex picker after the Monday model-catalog-sync cron, zero rebuild. gemini/antigravity is NOT listed and has no `--model` (flagTemplate.gemini=[]) - its model is the stepwise planner's job - so it shows just 'Default (via planner)'.
